title: Rajas con Crema Roasted Pepper Cream

seo.title: Rajas con Crema Recipe: Easy Mexican Roasted Poblano Cream
seo.metaDescription: Discover the best Rajas con Crema recipe! This authentic Mexican dish features roasted poblano peppers in a rich, creamy sauce. Perfect as a side or vegetarian main.
longTailKeywords: how to make rajas con crema
rajas con crema step by step
seo.h1: Authentic Rajas con Crema Roasted Pepper Cream Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Rajas con Crema (Step-by-Step)
Expert Tips for Perfect Rajas con Crema
Serving Suggestions for Rajas con Crema
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy Rajas con Crema, a Mexican vegetarian dish with roasted poblano peppers and corn.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this vibrant and flavorful Rajas con Crema, a classic Mexican side dish or vegetarian main.

description: Rajas con Crema is a beloved Mexican vegetarian dish, featuring tender strips of roasted poblano peppers bathed in a rich, creamy sauce. This comforting classic is enhanced with sweet corn kernels and often a touch of onion, creating a harmonious blend of flavors and textures. It is incredibly versatile, perfect as a hearty side dish or a delightful main course when served with warm tortillas or rice.

instructions.title: Roast the Poblano Peppers
instructions.text: Place poblano peppers directly over an open flame on a gas stove, under a broiler, or on a hot comal. Roast, turning frequently, until the skin is charred and blistered all over. This usually takes about 10-15 minutes.

instructions.title: Sweat the Peppers
instructions.text: Transfer the roasted poblanos to a bowl and cover tightly with plastic wrap, or place them in a paper bag and seal. Let them steam for 15 minutes. This step helps loosen the charred skin, making it easier to peel.

instructions.title: Peel and Slice the Peppers
instructions.text: Once cool enough to handle, carefully peel off the charred skin from the poblanos. Remove the stems and seeds, then slice the peppers into thin strips, which are known as rajas.

instructions.title: Sauté the Aromatics
instructions.text: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the thinly sliced white onion and cook until it softens and becomes translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer
instructions.text: Add the prepared poblano rajas and corn kernels to the skillet with the onions and garlic. Cook for 3-5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together. Pour in the Mexican crema (or heavy cream) and milk. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, stirring occasionally, and cook for 5-7 minutes until the sauce slightly thickens. Avoid boiling vigorously. Remove from heat and serve hot, garnished with crumbled queso fresco or cotija cheese if desired.

tips: For a deeper smoky flavor, ensure the poblano peppers are well charred but not burnt. The steaming step is crucial for easy peeling and to soften the peppers.
If Mexican crema is unavailable, heavy cream is a good substitute. For a slightly lighter version, you can use half-and-half, but be careful not to boil it vigorously to prevent curdling.
Rajas con Crema is fantastic served with warm corn tortillas, as a filling for tacos or quesadillas, or alongside rice and beans. It also makes a wonderful topping for enchiladas or chilaquiles.
Adjust the heat by adding a finely chopped serrano or jalapeño pepper along with the onion if you prefer a spicier dish.
article: Rajas con Crema Roasted Pepper Cream is a beloved Mexican Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.

to taste freshly ground black pepper
